notable_pick_trades: Traded Adam Thielen to Vikings for 2026 5th-rounder and 2027 4th-rounder; gave up conditional 2026 7th-rounder and 2027 5th-rounder
roster_context: NFC South champions at 8-9 in 2025, lost in Wild Card round to Rams. Bryce Young showed growth (3,011 passing yards) and Tetairoa McMillan won Offensive Rookie of the Year (70 catches, 1,014 yards). Team aggressively addressed pass rush and LB in free agency with Jaelan Phillips and Devin Lloyd. Lost Rico Dowdle, Cade Mays, and A'Shawn Robinson. Ikem Ekwonu ruptured patellar tendon in playoff loss, expected to miss most/all of 2026. Signed Rasheed Walker as 1-year LT replacement. Team is ascending but still has holes at DL depth, safety, WR2/WR3, and along the OL long-term.

needs.explanation: Released A'Shawn Robinson ($10.5M cap savings), losing the team's second-most productive DL behind Derrick Brown (62.2% of defensive snaps in 2025). Tershawn Wharton dealt with injuries all year (9 games played) and Bobby Brown III is a limited nose tackle. Fifth-round pick Cam Jackson is raw. Need a legitimate rotational or starting interior DL who can absorb snaps alongside Brown.
needs.current_starter: Derrick Brown (DE), Tershawn Wharton (DT), Bobby Brown III (NT)
needs.depth: Cam Jackson (2025 5th-rounder, raw), LaBryan Ray (rotational), Jaden Crumedy. Very thin after releasing Robinson.

needs.explanation: Nick Scott was re-signed on a 1-year, $3.25M deal but is 30 and viewed as upgradeable despite 111 tackles. Tre'von Moehrig is the anchor but graded poorly in coverage. Lathan Ransom showed flashes as a rookie (51 tackles, INT) but is a similar skill set to Moehrig — both are box/run-support types. Ejiro Evero's big nickel scheme needs three safeties, and the Panthers lack a true center-field free safety.
needs.depth: Lathan Ransom (2nd year), Demani Richardson, Isaiah Simmons (hybrid/ST). No true coverage FS on roster.

needs.explanation: Ikem Ekwonu suffered a ruptured patellar tendon in the Wild Card game and is expected to miss most or all of 2026. Signed Rasheed Walker (1-year, $10M) as a stopgap LT, but Walker is on a short-term deal. RT Taylor Moton is aging and entering the back end of his contract. Yosh Nijman surprisingly retired. Stone Forsythe is emergency depth only. Long-term OT investment needed even with Walker filling the 2026 gap.
needs.depth: Stone Forsythe (swing tackle depth), Ikem Ekwonu (on 5th-year option but rehabbing torn patellar tendon all year)

needs.explanation: Tetairoa McMillan is the clear WR1 (OROY) and Jalen Coker emerged late (19 catches, 244 yards, 3 TDs in final 5 games). But after those two, the cupboard is thin. Xavier Legette has massively underperformed as a 2024 1st-round pick with worsening PFF grades. John Metchie III was signed on a 1-year deal but has only 73 career NFL catches. Jimmy Horn Jr. is a gadget/speed player, not a reliable WR3. Need a true complement to McMillan.
needs.current_starter: Tetairoa McMillan (WR1), Jalen Coker (WR2), Xavier Legette (WR3)
needs.depth: John Metchie III (1-year deal), Jimmy Horn Jr., David Moore (mentor role, 1 catch in 2025), Brycen Tremayne

needs.explanation: Signed Jaelan Phillips to a massive 4-year, $120M deal — he immediately becomes the top pass rusher. But Phillips carries significant injury history. Nic Scourton (2nd-round pick, 5 sacks as rookie) and Princely Umanmielen provide young depth. DJ Wonnum and Trevis Gipson left in free agency. Patrick Jones II and Thomas Incoom are rotation pieces. Could still use another EDGE for insurance and rotation behind Phillips.
needs.depth: Princely Umanmielen, Patrick Jones II, Thomas Incoom. Phillips' injury history warrants depth investment.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 (if elite prospect falls to 19, take BPA)

needs.explanation: Ja'Tavion Sanders was placed on IR late in 2025 and has shown worsening PFF grades two years running. Tommy Tremble had back surgery in 2025 and is a better blocker than receiver. James Mitchell is an RFA depth piece. Bryce Young needs a reliable middle-of-field weapon. Multiple mock drafts have the Panthers targeting TE Kenyon Sadiq at 19.
needs.depth: Ja'Tavion Sanders (coming off IR, disappointing), James Mitchell (RFA). No reliable receiving TE on roster.

needs.explanation: Lost starting C Cade Mays to Lions (3-year, $25M) and Austin Corbett to free agency. Signed Luke Fortner (1-year, $4.75M) from Saints as center replacement. Guards Damien Lewis and Robert Hunt (returning from torn biceps) are strong starters, but Hunt missed 15 games in 2025. Chandler Zavala ended season on IR. Fortner is a stopgap — long-term center solution may be needed.
needs.current_starter: Damien Lewis (LG), Luke Fortner (C), Robert Hunt (RG)
needs.depth: Chandler Zavala (coming off IR), limited beyond starters. Brady Christensen and Jake Curhan left in FA.

needs.explanation: Lost Rico Dowdle (1,076 rushing yards, 297 receiving yards) to Pittsburgh. Chuba Hubbard becomes the lead back again. Trevor Etienne is a capable backup. Jonathon Brooks (2024 2nd-rounder) is returning from back-to-back ACL tears — only 9 career carries — and is a huge question mark. Could use a complementary back in mid-to-late rounds.
needs.depth: Trevor Etienne, Jonathon Brooks (returning from 2nd ACL tear, major question mark), Montrell Johnson Jr.

needs.explanation: Signed Devin Lloyd (3-year, $45M) — the top off-ball LB in free agency with 5 INTs in 2025. This was a massive upgrade. Trevin Wallace (season-ending shoulder surgery) returns for Year 3. Lost Christian Rozeboom (team tackles leader) in free agency. Claudin Cherelus stepped in as starter late. Could still add depth behind Lloyd and Wallace, but this is no longer a glaring need.
needs.current_starter: Devin Lloyd (signed FA), Trevin Wallace (returning from shoulder surgery)
needs.depth: Claudin Cherelus (started 4 games), Isaiah Simmons (hybrid), Jacoby Windmon. Much improved after Lloyd signing.

free_agency_impact: Signed EDGE Jaelan Phillips (4yr/$120M, $80M gtd) — transforms pass rush, reduces EDGE from critical to moderate depth need
Signed LB Devin Lloyd (3yr/$45M, $25M gtd) — fills biggest defensive void at off-ball LB, had 5 INTs in 2025
Signed QB Kenny Pickett (1yr/$7.5M) — backup to Bryce Young, replacing Andy Dalton
Signed OT Rasheed Walker (1yr/$10M) — starting LT while Ikem Ekwonu recovers from ruptured patellar tendon
Signed C Luke Fortner (1yr/$4.75M) — replaces Cade Mays who left for Lions
Signed OT Stone Forsythe (1yr) — emergency swing tackle depth
Signed WR John Metchie III (1yr) — former Bryce Young Alabama teammate, WR depth
Re-signed S Nick Scott (1yr/$3.25M) — returning starter at FS, still upgradeable
Re-signed S/LB Isaiah Simmons, CB Akayleb Evans, OLB Thomas Incoom, DE LaBryan Ray, CB Robert Rochell
Lost RB Rico Dowdle to Pittsburgh — had back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons
Lost C Cade Mays to Detroit Lions (3yr/$25M) — starting center departed
Lost OL Austin Corbett, Brady Christensen, Jake Curhan — OL depth gutted
Lost OLB DJ Wonnum, OLB Trevis Gipson, LB Christian Rozeboom, CB Damarri Mathis — defensive depth losses
Released DT A'Shawn Robinson — saved $10.5M cap, creates IDL need
OT Yosh Nijman retired — surprise retirement deepened OT need
Extended CB Jaycee Horn (4yr extension through 2029) — CB is NOT a need
scheme_fit_notes: DC Ejiro Evero runs a 3-4 base with flexibility to shift to a 4-3. Heavy use of a big nickel package requiring three safeties on the field. Moehrig plays as a hybrid box/LB role, so the third safety needs to be a true center-field FS. Phillips fits as a 3-4 OLB/EDGE similar to his role under Vic Fangio in Philly. IDL prospects who can play 3-tech and generate interior pressure are especially valuable given the loss of Robinson. The offense under Dave Canales is a West Coast system that values YAC and middle-of-field passing — a receiving TE (like Sadiq) would be a scheme-perfect fit.
